plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL, TQQQ) to zero.
Trim SPY to 20% and QQQ to 10% to reduce broad index concentration.
Reduce CRWD to 5% to lower tech focus.
Add QQQI to 15% to increase income exposure.
Hold remaining cash for a buffer.
Maintain tight stops on all positions.
conservative.read: This plan prioritizes capital preservation with a target of around 1.5% monthly growth. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration risk, aiming for lower drawdowns. Expect slower growth due to reduced risk-taking.

plan: Trim SOXL to 2% and sell SPAL, adding TQQQ to 5% for controlled leverage.
Trim SPY to 25% to address concentration.
Add QQQI to 10% for income diversification.
Trim SMH to 5% to reduce semiconductor concentration.
Add ARM as a new quality tech position at 5%.
Maintain a moderate cash buffer around 10%.
balanced.read: This plan aims for risk-first growth, targeting about 2.5% monthly. It balances broad index exposure with quality tech and controlled leverage. Expect moderate growth with managed drawdowns. Leveraged ETF decay is a factor to monitor.

plan: Increase SOXL to 10%, add SPAL to 5% and TQQQ to 10% for aggressive leveraged exposure.
Trim SPY to 20% and QQQ to 10% to reduce broad index holdings.
Trim SMH to 5% to decrease semiconductor concentration.
Add ARM as a new high-conviction tech position at 10%.
Maintain a small cash buffer around 5%.
Use wider but strict stops on all positions.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets substantial growth, around 4% monthly, by leaning into leverage and concentration. Expect higher potential gains but also significantly larger drawdowns and amplified losses due to leverage and leveraged ETF decay. Strict stop discipline is critical.

plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL, TQQQ) and high-beta tech (CRWD, SMH).
Increase SPY to 30% for broad market exposure.
Increase QQQI to 40% to maximize income generation.
Hold remaining positions at minimal allocation or zero.
Prioritize stability and monthly distributions over growth.
Maintain a cash buffer for yield reinvestment.
income.read: This income-focused plan aims for stable monthly distributions with a target of around 1% monthly. It significantly reduces volatility and growth potential by cutting leverage and high-beta names. While prioritizing income, NAV can still decline in market downturns.
